project.title: Proteomic analysis of bovine milk-derived extracellular vesicles isolated via salting-out
project.projectDescription: We developed a novel and cost-effective procedure to separate bovine milk-derived extracellular vesicles via salting-out and aimed to profile their proteomics as one important characterization in EV research.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: 1. Skimmed bovine milk, pasteurized and produced within three days, was stored below 4℃ and commercially available at a local grocery store. 2. Defatted milk was pre-warmed at 37℃ for 10 min. 3. Add 6 M hydrochloric acids (HCl) with pH value adjusted to 4.6 and the reaction lasts for 5 minutes. 4. Centrifugation: 5,000xg at 25℃ for 60 min to remove casein aggregates and other debris. The supernatant whey was collected. 5. To obtain different concentrations of ammonium sulfate: fine powder was added to 8 mL whey. The amount was measured using a calculator (link: https://www.encorbio.com/protocols/AM-SO4.htm).  6. The whey samples of 10 to 50% and those of 60 to 90% were kept at 4℃ for overnight and 60 min, respectively.  7. Centrifugation: 5,000xg at 4℃ for 60 min to remove whey proteins. 8. The supernatant in samples of 60 to 90% was collected and the pellets in samples of 10 to 50% were fully resuspended with 10 mL of 0.22-µm filtered sterile phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) 9. All samples were filtered with 0.45-µm and 0.22-µm membranes 10. Concentration and desalting: samples were washed with PBS and concentrated using centrifugal ultrafiltration devices (Cat. No. MAP100C38, 100k; Pall Corp., New York, NY, USA) at 5,000xg, 4℃ for 60 min to obtain the concentrated extracellular vesicles.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: According to the filter-aided sample preparation protocol, the proteins of mEVs were precipitated with acetone, reduced, alkylated with iodoacetamide, trypsinized, and desalted. Peptide mixtures were analyzed via liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (LC-MS) using Q Exactive Plus (ThermoFisher Scientific) coupled to Easy-nLC 1000 (ThermoFisher Scientific). Survey full scans MS spectra at a resolution of 70,000 were acquired across the 350-1600 m/z range in a data-dependent acquisition mode. The peptide identification was performed using Proteome Discoverer software, version 2.4 (ThermoFisher Scientific).

references.referenceLine: Tan XH, Fang D, Xu YD, Nan TG, Song WP, Gu YY, Gu SJ, Yuan YM, Xin ZC, Zhou LQ, Guan RL, Li XS. Skimmed Bovine Milk-Derived Extracellular Vesicles Isolated via "Salting-Out": Characterizations and Potential Functions as Nanocarriers. Front Nutr. 2021 8:769223

Characteristics[Specimen]: Skimmed bovine milk (Sanyuan Foods, Beijing, China)
Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic: Three milk samples of different expiration dates were used as biological replicates
Characteristics[Treatment]: 34% ammonium sulfate and 12-h salting-out were adopted in the following studies.
Comment[Instrument]: Q Exactive Plus (ThermoFisher Scientific, Waltham, MA, USA)
Acquity UPLC I-Class system (Waters Corp., Milford, MA, USA)
Comment[GradientTime]: The gradient condition was 0–0.5 min, 25% A; 0.5–4 min, 30% A; 4–7 min, 90% A; 7–9 min 25% A.
